codeReview.summary: The feature adds the intended shared config surface, documents it, and keeps `core` free of app-specific assets, but the base-URL validator still accepts malformed hostless HTTP(S) URIs instead of failing fast.

issues.description: The `parseBaseUrl` check only requires `URI.isAbsolute()` plus an `http`/`https` scheme, so values like `https:/demo.example.test` are accepted even though Java parses them with `host=null`. That violates the feature's fail-fast contract for invalid base URLs: callers will get a later navigation failure instead of an actionable configuration error.

summary: Reviewed the feature transcript skeleton, handoff, shared-state docs, and commit `6d3ce8bfbeff500b8eb86febb93117345df141cc`. The implementation is close, but it does not fully satisfy the invalid-base-URL fail-fast requirement because malformed hostless HTTP(S) URLs still load successfully.
